page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c5.4xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores utilizing the x86_64 architecture, specifically the Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at a base clock speed of 2.5 GHz. The instance is configured with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. There is no bundled local storage or GPU hardware included with this instance type. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 5 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is relatively weak, as shown by stress-ng and Geekbench single-core scores falling in the bottom 25%, and single-threaded gzip compression registering in the bottom 10%. However, multi-core execution scales to average levels, with stress-ng multi-core, Passmark CPU, and Geekbench multi-core scores all positioning in the middle 50%. Memory bandwidth tests for both cached small blocks and uncached large blocks also demonstrate average performance, alongside standard database operations and Redis SET operations.

Given its compute-focused resource ratio of 2 GB RAM per vCPU, this instance offers qualitative cost efficiency for parallelized workloads that do not require massive memory footprints or local storage. It is suitable for multi-threaded tasks such as software compilation, static web serving, and moderate multi-threaded decompression. It can also handle small-to-medium language model inference, though prompt processing on larger models is constrained by the single-core performance profile.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c5.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 16 dedicated Intel Xeon 8163 physical cores on the x86_64 architecture, running at 2.5 GHz. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM, providing 2.0 GB per core, with no local storage or GPU resources. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, the instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps. Benchmark data shows weak single-core performance, but multi-core execution, memory bandwidth, and database operations consistently reach average tiers. This resource profile makes the instance qualitatively cost-effective for parallelized, compute-bound workloads that do not demand high memory-to-CPU ratios. It is well-suited for multi-threaded software compilation, static web serving, and small-scale model inference.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c5.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 16 Intel Xeon cores, 32 GB RAM, and 5 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering average multi-core performance for parallel workloads.

tagline: A dedicated multi-core compute instance designed for parallel processing, software compilation, and web serving workloads without local storage requirements.
